title: First analysis | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Viewing your first analysis' results

## [Main Branch Status](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#main-branch-status "Main Branch Status")

The **Main Branch Status** is the quality gate of your main branch, indicating whether it meets your quality requirements and is ready to be released.

**The quality gate displays Not Computed because it needs to be configured.**

We strongly recommend that you set up your main branch quality gate. To do so, you must set a *new code definition*. Select **Set New Code Definition** to get started.

See the [Quality gate](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/quality-gates/ "Quality gate") and [About new code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/digging-deeper/clean-as-you-code/about-new-code/ "About new code") pagesfor more details.  Once you set it up, push a change to the main branch. A new analysis will run, and the quality gate status will display either **Passed** or **Failed**.

## [Main Branch Evolution](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#main-branch-evolution "Main Branch Evolution")

The **Main Branch Evolution** displays a summary of the code quality results from the main branch analysis. In this section, you will find tabs that display different metrics:

- **Issues** displays the number of issues found in the main branch.
- **Coverage** displays the percentage of testable code in the main branch that is covered by your test cases.
- **Duplications** displays the percentage of main branch code that is duplicated.

**Coverage displays zero percent because it needs to be configured.**

Initially, your coverage will display zero percent because it requires configuration. To set it up, see the [Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/ "Coverage") section.

Once it's configured, push a change to the main branch to update the analysis.  After the a new analysis is run, the coverage percentage will be displayed.

The **historical data** chart shows the progress of your code quality.

**To see historical data, you must have run at least two analyses.**

Push a change to your main branch. A new analysis will run, and the historical data will be displayed. In the **Main Branch Evolution** section, select **See full history** to the project's **Activity** page.

## [Latest Activity](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#latest-activity "Latest Activity")

Scroll down to find the **Latest Activity** section; it displays a feed of all analyses that have been run, including all main branch analyses, pull request analyses, and branch analyses.

## [Project navigation](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#project-navigation "Project navigation")

The project navigation on the left lets you move between the four views: **Overview**, **Main Branch**, **Pull Requests**, and **Branches**.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Project navigation](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/c9825d9b-5bfa-4ea0-9906-5732d942a915/project-navigation.png?w=1400&h=702&auto=format&fit=crop "Project navigation")

## [Main Branch](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#main-branch "Main Branch")

Select **Branches** > **MAIN BRANCH** to see a more fine-grained view of your most recent [Main branch analysis.](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/main-branch-analysis/ "Main branch analysis.")

## [Pull Requests](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#pull-requests "Pull Requests")

In addition to analyzing your main branch every time it changes, SonarQube Cloud also analyzes individual pull requests. These analyses run when a pull request is opened and on each change to the pull request branch. This all happens *before* you merge, letting you catch problems before they even get to the main branch, an important part of the [Clean as You Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/digging-deeper/clean-as-you-code/introduction/ "Clean as You Code") methodology. The results of pull request analysis are displayed in the **Pull Requests** view of your SonarQube Cloud project and the pull request view of your DevOps Platform (GitHub, Bitbucket Cloud, Azure DevOps, or GitLab).

See the [Pull request analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/pull-request-analysis/ "Pull request analysis") page for more details.

## [Branches](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started/first-analysis/#branches "Branches")

The **Branches** view displays all the non-pull request branches for which you have set up analysis. Initially, only the main branch is listed here. But, you can configure other branches to be analyzed. Once a branch is configured, an analysis is run on every change to that branch.

See [Branch analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/branch-analysis/ "Branch analysis") and [Branch analysis setup](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/branch-analysis-setup/ "Branch analysis setup").
